title: Agrostis salmantica
intro: Agrostis salmantica, also known as Salamantina grass or Common bent, is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to reduce pain and improve overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Agrostis salmantica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Agrostis salmantica is commonly used in traditional herbal medicine, and its extracts are often prepared as teas, infusions, or tinctures, as well as being used in topical applications such as creams and ointments.
taxonomy: Agrostis salmantica is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Agrostis, which is a group of grasses commonly known as bentgrasses. The family Poaceae is a large and diverse group of monocots that includes many different types of grasses. Agrostis salmantica is native to the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe. It is classified as a perennial grass, meaning it grows and blooms year after year.
